subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board
- **Game board** — Central board showing worker placement locations: Hunting Grounds, Forest, Clay Pit, Quarry, River (gold), Farm, Tool Maker, Mating Hut, Civilization Card slots (4), and Building tiles.

#### Per Player
- **Workers** — Start with 5 worker meeples (expandable to 10 maximum).
- **Player board** — Tracks food production (farm level), tools, and resource storage.
- **Score marker** — For the VP track.

#### Shared Components
- **Resources** — Wood, clay (brick), stone, gold tokens.
- **Food tokens** — Various denominations.
- **Dice** — 7 dice (shared among all players for resource gathering rolls).
- **Civilization cards** — 36 cards providing immediate bonuses and end-game multiplier scoring.
- **Building tiles** — 4 stacks of building tiles (worth VP when constructed).
- **Tool tokens** — Value 1 and 2 tool tiles for each player.
- **Farm track marker** — Tracks each player's agriculture level.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Board Setup
1. **Place the board** — Central board showing all placement locations.
2. **Civilization cards** — Shuffle and deal 4 face up in the card display slots. Stack remaining cards as a draw pile.
3. **Building stacks** — Create building tile stacks equal to the number of players. Each stack has 7 tiles. Reveal the top tile of each stack.
4. **Resource supply** — Place wood, clay, stone, gold, and food tokens near the board.
5. **Tool supply** — Place tool tokens near the board.

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **2 players** — 2 building stacks. Certain placement locations have reduced worker capacity.
- **3 players** — 3 building stacks.
- **4 players** — 4 building stacks. Full worker capacity at all locations.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Starting Items
1. **Workers** — Receive 5 worker meeples.
2. **Food** — Receive 12 food tokens.
3. **Player board** — Take a player board. Set farm track to 0.
4. **Tools** — Start with no tools (0/0/0 on tool slots).
5. **Score marker** — Place on 0 on the VP track.
6. **No starting resources** — Begin with no wood, clay, stone, or gold.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Player
- **Random selection** — Choose randomly. Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Starting State
- Each player: 5 workers, 12 food, no resources, no tools, farm at level 0.
- 4 civilization cards face up.
- Building stacks set up and top tiles revealed.
- All board locations are empty and available.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Place workers** — Starting with the first player and going clockwise, each player places 1 or more workers on a single location. Continue around the table until all players have placed all their workers.
2. **Resolve workers** — Starting with the first player, each player resolves all of their placed workers, one location at a time in any order.
3. **Feed workers** — Each player must pay 1 food per worker. Farm level reduces this need (farm produces food automatically). Workers you cannot feed cost 10 VP penalty.

#### Placement Rules
- **One location per placement** — On your turn during placement, you place workers at exactly 1 location.
- **Hunting Grounds** — Unlimited workers from any number of players.
- **Forest, Clay Pit, Quarry, River** — Maximum 7 workers total (across all players).
- **Farm** — Exactly 1 worker, 1 player per round.
- **Tool Maker** — Exactly 1 worker, 1 player per round.
- **Mating Hut** — Exactly 2 workers from 1 player per round.
- **Civilization Cards** — 1 worker per card slot, 1 player per slot.
- **Buildings** — 1 worker per building stack, 1 player per stack.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Resource Gathering (Dice-Based)
- **Roll dice** — Roll 1 die per worker placed at that resource location.
- **Divide total by resource cost:**
  - Food (Hunting Grounds): total / 2
  - Wood (Forest): total / 3
  - Clay (Clay Pit): total / 4
  - Stone (Quarry): total / 5
  - Gold (River): total / 6
- Round down. The result is how many of that resource you receive.
- **Tools** — After rolling, you may add tool values to the total (each tool used once per round, then flipped to used side).

#### Farm
- Place 1 worker to increase your farm level by 1 (maximum 10).
- Each farm level produces 1 food automatically during the feeding phase, reducing or eliminating the need to hunt.

#### Tool Maker
- Place 1 worker to gain 1 tool level. Tools fill 3 slots on your player board (each can hold values 1-4, for a maximum of 12 total tool value).

#### Mating Hut
- Place 2 workers to gain 1 new worker (added to your supply for next round). Maximum 10 workers.

#### Civilization Cards
- Place 1 worker on a card slot. Pay resources equal to the card's position (1st slot = top resource on card, 2nd slot = top 2 resources, etc.) to claim the card.
- Cards provide immediate bonuses (resources, tools, food, VP) and end-game scoring multipliers.

#### Buildings
- Place 1 worker on a building stack. Pay the resource combination shown on the top tile to score its VP immediately.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Tools
- **3 tool slots** — Each slot holds a tool of increasing value (1, then upgraded to 2, then 3, then 4).
- **Usage** — When gathering resources, add any number of your tool values to the dice total. Each tool can only be used once per round.
- **Flip to used** — After using a tool, flip it to show it is spent for the round. Tools reset at the start of the next round.

#### Feeding
- **1 food per worker** — You must pay food equal to your number of workers.
- **Farm production** — Your farm level provides that many free food each round.
- **Starvation penalty** — If you cannot feed all workers, you lose 10 VP (you may choose to starve intentionally). You never lose workers.

#### Civilization Card Multipliers
- Cards show symbols for end-game scoring:
  - **Green (tool multiplier)** — VP = tool level x number of these cards.
  - **Green (farmer multiplier)** — VP = farm level x number of these cards.
  - **Green (builder multiplier)** — VP = number of buildings x number of these cards.
  - **Green (shaman multiplier)** — VP = number of workers x number of these cards.
  - **Sand (unique set)** — Collect unique background patterns for escalating VP (1/4/9/16/25/36... = n-squared).

#### Dice Probability
- Average die roll is 3.5 per die. With 3 workers at the Quarry (stone, cost 5): expected 3.5 x 3 / 5 = 2.1 stone = 2 stone.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End Trigger
- The game ends when either: at least 1 civilization card stack is exhausted (cannot refill to 4 cards), OR at least 1 building tile stack is exhausted.
- Finish the current round (all players complete placement, resolution, and feeding).

#### Final Scoring
1. **In-game VP** — VP accumulated from buildings and card bonuses during play.
2. **Civilization card multipliers** — Calculate end-game scoring from all collected civilization cards.
3. **Remaining resources** — Each resource (wood, clay, stone, gold) is worth 1 VP. Each food is worth 1 VP.

#### Winner
- The player with the most total VP wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Most total resources + food remaining. Then most farm level + tool level.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game
- **Mating Hut first** — Getting to 10 workers as fast as possible gives you more actions every round. Workers are the most important early investment.
- **Farm early** — Each farm level saves 1 food per round for the rest of the game. The compounding value is enormous.
- **Tool Maker** — Tools improve every dice roll for the entire game. Getting to tool level 3-4 early is very efficient.
- **Grab multiplier cards** — Civilization cards with multipliers (tools, farms, buildings, workers) are most powerful when collected early, as you build toward maximizing the multiplier.
- **Do not over-hunt** — Food from hunting is inefficient compared to farming. Transition to farm-based food as soon as possible.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Staying at 5 workers** — Players who never visit the Mating Hut fall behind on actions. More workers = more resources and more options.
- **Ignoring tools** — Without tools, dice rolls are unpredictable. Tools smooth out variance and increase average yields.
- **Not diversifying civilization cards** — The unique-set scoring (n-squared) scales incredibly well. Even 1-2 unique cards are efficient.
- **Over-investing in gold** — Gold (divide by 6) is very unreliable without tools and many workers. Focus on wood and clay early.
- **Starving intentionally too often** — The 10 VP penalty is steep. Build farm level to avoid this.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Critical Choices
- **Workers vs. tools vs. farm** — The three permanent investments compete for the same worker slots. Balance based on what opponents leave open.
- **Buildings vs. civilization cards** — Buildings give immediate VP but no end-game bonus. Civilization cards give smaller immediate effects but can generate huge end-game multipliers.
- **Resource allocation** — Spend resources on buildings for VP now, or save for expensive civilization cards with better multipliers.
- **When to use tools** — Tools are once per round. Use them on your most important gathering roll (typically the resource you need most for a building or card purchase).
- **Blocking opponents** — Placing workers on the Farm, Tool Maker, or Mating Hut before opponents can deny them critical upgrades.
